title: DS18B20 1-Wire Digital Temperature Sensing
content: The DS18B20 is a 1-Wire digital temperature sensor providing 9–12 bit resolution over a single data line. Temperature range: −55 °C to +125 °C; accuracy ±0.5 °C from −10 °C to +85 °C. Multiple sensors share one MCU GPIO via unique 64-bit ROM addresses.
extended_content: 1-Wire protocol: master initiates reset pulse, then ROM commands (SEARCH_ROM, MATCH_ROM, SKIP_ROM) to address individual sensors on a bus. Parasite power mode draws operating current from the data line via an internal capacitor, eliminating the need for a separate supply wire. Conversion time: 9-bit = 93.75 ms; 12-bit = 750 ms. Typical wiring: 4.7 kΩ pull-up resistor on data line to VCC (3.3 V or 5 V). Available in waterproof stainless-steel probe form for liquid and soil measurements. Arduino: Dallas Temperature library simplifies multi-sensor enumeration and reading.
source: Maxim DS18B20 Programmable Resolution 1-Wire Digital Thermometer Datasheet, Rev 3
